test_title: B-NLS low-density 'hole' — quantum pressure singularity stress test
stress_question: How small can N get before the chosen method fails? Madelung-Psi should handle this well; direct (N, phi) almost certainly fails.

methods_tried.method_name: direct (u, N, phi_tilde) RK4 spectral, eps_reg=1e-6
methods_tried.failure_mode: Catastrophic dispersive cascade: spectral oscillations push min(N) below 0 around t=0.0245. Once N<0, sqrt(N+eps) saturates at sqrt(eps)=1e-3 and Q=(sqrt(N))_xx/(2 sqrt(N)) carries no physical meaning, but its numerical value explodes by 5 orders of magnitude in ~10 dt. This back-reacts through phi_t -> phi_x_t -> u_t and the entire state blows up by t=0.027.
methods_tried.evidence: min_N history shows monotonic decrease 1.0e-3 -> 5e-4 -> 0 -> -8e-5 over 50 dt steps, then exponential blowup. Mass conservation is exact up to the blowup moment (so this is NOT a continuity-equation problem; it is a Q-singularity problem).

methods_tried.method_name: direct (u, N, phi_tilde) RK4 spectral, eps_reg=1e-3
methods_tried.failure_mode: Same dispersive cascade as M1 but slower. Raising eps_reg to 1e-3 (matching the initial background) flattens Q at t=0 and delays the moment when min_N first crosses 0 from 0.0245 to 0.032, but does not prevent the crossing. Once N is below 0, the regularization eps cannot prevent Q from blowing up because the spectral derivative (sqrt(N+eps))_xx is itself oscillatory when N is at the noise floor.
methods_tried.evidence: Comparing E1 vs E2: identical mass conservation, identical pre-blowup dynamics shape, just rescaled in time by ~1.6x. This is a structural failure mode that no eps choice cures.

params.scheme: Strang split: L(dt/2) N(dt) L(dt/2). Linear half-step exp(-i (k^2/2) dt/2) in Fourier (gives -Q in phi_t). Nonlinear full step exp(-i V_nl dt) pointwise, with V_nl = -2 kappa(|psi|^2 - eps_mad) - u*(c+phi_tilde_x) - 0.5(c+phi_tilde_x)^2. u updated by RK4 of m-equation with frozen N, phi_x.

methods_tried.evidence: Madelung representation enforces |psi|^2 >= 0 by construction, so N = |psi|^2 - eps_mad >= -eps_mad analytically under exact unitary evolution. Numerically min_N stays at floor -eps_mad = -1e-3 (only briefly touches -9.999e-4 at noise floor). Q_max bounded around 1.7e3 (transient, never runaway). Mass drift 0.09% is from the split-step truncation error in advecting |psi|^2 by u, not from any singular behavior.

positive_findings: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step at dt=5e-4, eps_mad=1e-3 completes T=4 on the S8 low-density problem with mass drift 0.09% and min_N always at the -eps_mad floor (i.e. effectively zero physical density).

Splitting phi = c*x + phi_tilde (periodic) is REQUIRED for any spectral method on this problem — without it spectral derivatives of phi_0=0.1*x produce Gibbs oscillations with phi_x range [-17, +8] instead of constant 0.1, and every method fails immediately for the wrong reason.
2/3 dealiasing on the nonlinear products (u*m, u*phi_x, phi_x^2, (u+phi_x)*N) is necessary even for Madelung — without it the u-equation accumulates high-k errors.

Madelung-Psi keeps Q_max bounded (1.76e3 peak) even when min(|psi|^2) saturates at the eps_mad floor — i.e., the quantum-pressure-from-(sqrt(N))_xx singularity is fully cured at the representation level, not just regularized.
negative_findings: Direct (N, phi) RK4 spectral with eps_reg=1e-6 FAILS at t=0.027 for the S8 IC (min_N0=1e-3): first N<0 happens at t=0.0245 with value -7.9e-5, then catastrophic blowup with max|Q|=1.08e5 within 10 more steps.

Direct (N, phi) RK4 spectral with eps_reg=1e-3 FAILS at t=0.044 — only 1.6x delay relative to eps=1e-6. Raising eps_reg merely postpones the blowup; it does not cure the structural problem that spectral dispersion drives N below 0 when min(N) ~ background_level.

Direct (N, phi) without N-positivity-preserving structure cannot evolve a soliton-on-background with min(N0) <= 1e-3 to any non-trivial time on this problem class, regardless of eps_reg in {1e-6, 1e-3}. The quantum pressure (sqrt(N))_xx/(2 sqrt(N)) is not the immediate cause — the immediate cause is that N drops below 0 due to dispersive oscillations, after which Q is meaningless.

The failure mode is INDEPENDENT of mass conservation: in both direct experiments, mass remained exact at 2.030 right up to the moment N went negative. This is therefore a sign-preservation failure, not a flux-divergence failure.

The quantum-pressure singularity is the central new failure mode in B-NLS that is absent in BKdV (which has no Q term). For any IC with min(N) below ~1e-2 of the peak, direct (N, phi) is structurally non-viable and Madelung-Psi must be used.

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: min_N >> background, i.e. min_N / max_N > ~0.1 throughout (untested here, see other S tests)
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: min_N / max_N <= 1e-3 (this test) — fails within 50 time steps regardless of eps_reg
parameter_boundaries.evidence: S8 IC has min_N0/max_N0 = 1e-3 and both eps=1e-6 and eps=1e-3 fail before t=0.05

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: no value of eps_reg in [1e-6, 1e-3] gives stability on this problem
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: all tested values fail; the failure time only scales as ~1.6x when going from 1e-6 to 1e-3

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: min_N >= -eps_mad by construction; tested down to min_N = -1.0e-3 with eps_mad = 1e-3
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: not observed for T=4; the structure |psi|^2 >= 0 is preserved by unitary split-step
parameter_boundaries.evidence: min_N history saturates at -eps_mad floor throughout T=4 with no instability; mass drift 0.09%

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: eps_mad >= O(noise floor), eps_mad <= O(background). 1e-3 (matching background) works.
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: untested in this run; predicted that eps_mad too small (~1e-12) could cause cancellation in N = |psi|^2 - eps_mad

central_finding_for_paper: The quantum-pressure term (sqrt(N))_xx/(2 sqrt(N)) is the central new failure mode of B-NLS relative to BKdV. Direct (N, phi) formulations fail catastrophically within ~1% of T_final when min(N) approaches the background level, because spectral dispersion drives N below zero at the noise floor. Regularization via eps in sqrt(N+eps) does not cure this — it merely delays failure by a factor of ~1.6 per 3 orders of magnitude in eps. The Madelung-Psi representation (psi = sqrt(N + eps_mad) e^{i phi}) handles the singularity at the level of representation: |psi|^2 = N + eps_mad >= 0 is preserved structurally by unitary split-step integration, and the quantum pressure becomes a regular linear half-step in Fourier space. On the S8 IC, Madelung-Psi achieves min_N at the -eps_mad floor (-1e-3) with 0.09% mass drift over T=4, while direct methods at the same dt blow up at t=0.027 (eps=1e-6) and t=0.044 (eps=1e-3).
method_recommendation: For B-NLS problems with min(N) below ~1% of the peak density, Madelung-Psi with Strang split-step is the only viable method tested. Direct (N, phi) is appropriate only when N is safely bounded away from zero throughout the simulation.
